description: Regression for the 'consolidate onto a terrain objective' bug. A unit standing ON a large central terrain objective (14.01) controls it — MissionManager counts a model as in range once its base overlaps the hosting area — but ConsolidationMove used to measure only model-edge -> marker CENTRE vs 3"+20mm. On a ~11" wide central ruin a unit's bases sit >3.78" from the marker point, so the Consolidate step told the player 'no consolidation mode applies — the unit cannot move' even though the unit was on the objective. Both now share MissionManager.model_in_objective_range. This scenario converts obj_center into its terrain-hosted form (loads the take_and_hold_mirror layout the game already ships for this matchup and rebuilds the board objectives with source_pieces), stands the isolated Shield-Captain in a clear patch of that central area — 4.9" from the marker centre, no enemies near — passes the global Pile In step with real END_PILE_IN actions, enters the real Consolidate step, hands the active player's (P2) half over with a real END_CONSOLIDATION, then on P1's half asserts: (1) the ConsolidationStepDialog the player sees lists the Shield-Captain with the '[Objective ...]' hint — proof select_mode returned 'objective' for a model 4.9" from the marker, which only a terrain-aware measurement yields (open ground reads '[No move possible from here]'); (2) opening the real ConsolidateDialog and validating a real toward-objective move is accepted — the gate that raised the red 'no consolidation mode applies' error before the fix. Setup uses only single-expression execute_script calls against production methods; the pile-in and consolidate steps, the picker, and the move dialog are the real player UI driven through the real action pipeline.
